reviews.label: Columbia
reviews.description: Eli and the Thirteenth Confession is the second album by New York City-born singer, songwriter, and pianist Laura Nyro, released in 1968.
reviews.context: Laura Nyro emerged in the late 1960s as a singular voice, blending soul, pop and Broadway into a sound that would influence countless artists. This record captures her at a creative peak.
reviews.introduction: Eli and the Thirteenth Confession remains one of the most ambitious and emotionally charged albums of its era. Here we break down its themes and standout moments.

breakdown.title: Side A
breakdown.content: The opening run of songs establishes Nyro's gift for **melodic storytelling** and bold arrangements. *Eli's Comin'* became a hit for Three Dog Night and remains a staple of the era.
The title track and *Stoned Soul Picnic* showcase her ability to move between intimacy and grandeur, with lush orchestration supporting her piano and voice.

reviews.conclusion: Eli and the Thirteenth Confession stands as a landmark of 1960s pop and soul. Its influence can be heard in the work of Todd Rundgren, Kate Bush and many others who followed.

comments.text: Beautiful record. Nyro's songwriting is unmatched.
Arrived at this via Three Dog Night. The original is even better.
